REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT MARK BRUNIG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ALLEGE FACTS IN SUPPORT OF THE CAUSES OF ACTION SUBJECT TO THIS DEMURRER. PLAINTIFF SHALL HAVE 10 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. LATE-FILED OPPOSITION IS STRICKEN. FURTHERMORE, A HEARING IS SET FOR JUNE 23, 2022 FOR PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL, DENNIS PHILLIPS, TO ADDRESS WHY AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE SHALL NOT ISSUE FOR PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO PARTICIPATE IN GOOD FAITH IN THE MEET AND CONFER PROCESS REQUIRED UNDER CCP 430.41. DENNIS PHILLIPS TO FILE A RESPONSE NO LATER THAN JUNE 16, 2022. IF COUNSEL'S EXPLANATION IS SATISFACTORY, THE HEARING WILL BE TAKEN OFF CALENDAR. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-22-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT MARK BRUNIG MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. Amended Notice of Motion on file. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

Defendant Mark Brunig'S Amended Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-17-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Parisa Saleki; Freeman Mathis & Gary, LLP; 415-627-9000; appearing on behalf of defendant Mark Brunig.

No other appearances. Defendant's counsel states that she contests the court's tentative ruling, but that she did not give timely notice that she would be appearing to contest. Notice was given around 6pm the night before the hearing. The court explains that no argument can be heard without the other side present if timely notice was not provided. The court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MAR BRUNIG'S AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED. Allegations in the complaint, which must be taken as true for the purposes of this motion, are sufficient to support claims of emotional distress and punitive damages.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Off Calendar Jun-23-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows: HEARING ON ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: FAILURE TO PARTICIPATE IN MEET AND CONFER is OFF CALENDAR. Response filed on June 16, 2022. The Court notes that business e-mail communications should be checked at least once a day if an e-mail address is provided for communication to the public, the Court or other litigants.
Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-19-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances by any attorney, any party or any interested party.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MARK BRUNIG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts in support of the causes of action subject to this demurrer. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. Late-filed opposition is stricken. Furthermore, a hearing is set for June 23, 2022 for Plaintiff's counsel, Dennis Phillips, to address why an order to show cause shall not issue for Plaintiff's failure to participate in good faith in the meet and confer process required under CCP 430.41. Dennis Phillips to file a response no later than June 16, 2022. If counsel's explanation is satisfactory, the hearing will be taken off calendar. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)
